author: Isaac Asimov

text: Writing, to me, is simply thinking through my fingers.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: A subtle thought that is in error may yet give rise to fruitful inquiry that can establish truths of great value.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: To be sure, nothing is more gentle than fire when it burns; nothing warmer than ice when it freezes; nothing kinder than a laser when it cuts.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: The only constant is change, continuing change, inevitable change.
author: Isaac Asimov

text: Any planet is Earth to those that live on it.
author: Isaac Asimov — The Naked Sun

text: You have the right to perform your actions, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 2.47

text: Change is the law of the universe. You can be a millionaire, or a pauper, in an instant.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: The soul is never born nor dies at any time. It has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 2.20

text: Let right deeds be thy motive, not the fruit which comes from them.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: A person can rise through the efforts of his own mind; or draw himself down, in the same manner. Because each person is his own friend or enemy.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 6.5

text: Fear not what is not real, never was and never will be. What is real, always was and cannot be destroyed.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: The mind is everything. What you think, you become. What you feel, you attract. What you imagine, you create.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: Set your heart upon your work but never its reward.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: Do not be led by others, awaken your own mind, amass your own experience, and decide for yourself your own path.
author: Atharva Veda

text: Truth is one; the sages call it by many names.
author: Rig Veda — 1.164.46

text: From the unreal lead me to the real. From darkness lead me to light. From death lead me to immortality.
author: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad

text: Aham Brahmasmi — I am Brahman. I am the infinite reality.
author: Brihadaranyaka Upanishad — 1.4.10

text: Tat Tvam Asi — Thou art that.
author: Chandogya Upanishad — 6.8.7

text: The Self is the friend of a man who masters himself through the Self, but for a man without self-mastery, the Self is like an enemy at war.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 6.6

text: When a person responds to the joys and sorrows of others as if they were his own, he has attained the highest state of spiritual union.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 6.32

text: The wise see knowledge and action as one; they see truly.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 5.4

text: He who has no attachments can really love others, for his love is pure and divine.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: You cannot believe in God until you believe in yourself.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Take up one idea. Make that one idea your life — think of it, dream of it, live on that idea.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: The greatest sin is to think yourself weak.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: In a conflict between the heart and the brain, follow your heart.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Arise, awake and stop not till the goal is reached.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: All power is within you; you can do anything and everything.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: We are what our thoughts have made us; so take care about what you think. Words are secondary. Thoughts live; they travel far.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Talk to yourself once in a day, otherwise you may miss meeting an excellent person in this world.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: They alone live who live for others; the rest are more dead than alive.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: The world is the great gymnasium where we come to make ourselves strong.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Do not wait for anybody or anything. Do whatever you can, build your hope on none.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Purity, patience and perseverance are the three essentials to success and above all, love.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: The moment I have realized God sitting in the temple of every human body, the moment I stand in reverence before every human being and see God in him — that moment I am free from bondage.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Where can we go to find God if we cannot see Him in our own hearts and in every living being?
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: The fire that warms us can also consume us; it is not the fault of the fire.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: It is our own mental attitude which makes the world what it is for us. Our thoughts make things beautiful, our thoughts make things ugly.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: We reap what we sow. We are the makers of our own fate.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: God is to be worshipped as the one beloved, dearer than everything in this and next life.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: A man is not poor without a rupee but a man is really poor without a dream and ambition.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: In one word, this ideal is that you are divine.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Conquer yourself and the whole universe is yours.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Strength is life, weakness is death.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Each soul is potentially divine. The goal is to manifest this divinity within.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: If you think you are bound, you remain bound; you make your own bondage. If you know you are free, you are free this moment.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Meditation can turn fools into sages but unfortunately fools never meditate.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Neither seek nor avoid, take what comes.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: You must have an iron will if you would cross the ocean. You must be strong enough to pierce mountains.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: The whole secret of existence is to have no fear.
author: Swami Vivekananda

text: Yoga is the journey of the self, through the self, to the self.
author: Bhagavad Gita

text: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his very mind will be the greatest enemy.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 6.6

text: One who sees inaction in action, and action in inaction, is intelligent among men.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 4.18

text: The soul which is not moved, the soul that with strong and constant calm takes sorrow and takes joy indifferently, lives in the life undying.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 2.15

text: As a lamp in a windless place does not waver, so the transcendentalist, whose mind is controlled, remains always steady in his meditation on the transcendent self.
author: Bhagavad Gita — 6.19

text: What is here is also there; what is there is also here. Who sees multiplicity but not the one indivisible Self must wander on and on from death to death.
author: Katha Upanishad — 2.1.10

text: The Self cannot be known through the study of scriptures, nor through subtlety of the intellect, nor through much learning; but by him who longs for it — by the Self is the Self understood.
author: Katha Upanishad — 1.2.23

text: Two birds sit on the same tree. One eats fruit, the other watches silently. So too are two aspects of the self — one experiencing, one witnessing.
author: Mundaka Upanishad — 3.1.1

text: The Atman is never born and never dies. It is not a product of anything, nor is it the cause of anything. It is without birth, without change, without death — the eternal, ever-present Self.
author: Katha Upanishad

text: That which permeates all, which nothing transcends and which, like the universal space around us, fills everything completely from within and without, that Supreme non-dual Brahman — that thou art.
author: Sankaracharya — Vivekachudamani

text: The universe is the externalization of the soul.
author: Adi Shankaracharya

text: Do not be proud of wealth, people, relations and friends, or youth. All these are snatched by time in the blink of an eye. Giving up this illusory world, know and attain the Supreme.
author: Adi Shankaracharya — Bhaja Govindam

text: Without self-knowledge, without understanding the working and functions of his mind, man cannot be free.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: It is no measure of health to be well adjusted to a profoundly sick society.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: The ability to observe without evaluating is the highest form of intelligence.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: If you begin to understand what you are without trying to change it, then what you are undergoes a transformation.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: The flowering of love is meditation.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: To understand the immeasurable, the mind must be extraordinarily quiet, still.
author: Jiddu Krishnamurti

text: In the beginner's mind there are many possibilities, but in the expert's mind there are few.
author: Shunryu Suzuki — Zen Mind, Beginner's Mind

text: The flame that burns twice as bright burns half as long. — Live fully but be mindful of your energy.
author: Tao Te Ching — Lao Tzu

text: Ekam sat vipra bahudha vadanti — Truth is one, the wise call it by many names.
author: Rig Veda

text: Sarve bhavantu sukhinah — May all beings be happy. May all be free from suffering.
author: Hindu Prayer — Brihadaranyaka Upanishad

text: We shouldn't worry about being able to achieve our dreams, as long as we have the desire to do it!
author: Monkey D. Luffy — One Piece

text: Inherited Will, the Destiny of the Age, and the Dreams of its People. These are things that will not be stopped. As long as people continue to pursue the meaning of freedom, these things will never cease to be!
author: Gol D. Roger — One Piece

text: If you don't risk your life, you can't create a future.
author: Monkey D. Luffy — One Piece

text: A man's dream will never die.
author: Whitebeard — One Piece

text: Miracles only occur for those with the determination to never stop trying!
author: Monkey D. Luffy — One Piece

text: A wound that'd make an ordinary man unconscious... I won't lose to it. A wound that would kill an ordinary person... I won't lose to it! To face one who is extraordinary, I can't allow myself to be ordinary!
author: Roronoa Zoro — One Piece

text: Our motto on hard days of training: 'To be stronger than yesterday.' If I have to, I'll be stronger than a half day ago, even a minute ago.
author: Rock Lee — Naruto

text: Sorry I'm late. I got lost on the road of life.
author: Kakashi Hatake — Naruto

text: I won't run away anymore... I won't go back on my word... that is my ninja way! To become Hokage is my dream!
author: Naruto Uzumaki — Naruto

text: A genius? So I was not born with a whole lot of natural talent. But I work hard and I never give up! That is my gift; that is my ninja way!
author: Rock Lee — Naruto

text: Never give up without even trying. Do what you can, no matter how small the effect it may have!
author: Obito Uchiha — Naruto

text: No job is beneath you. Be really great at whatever you start with.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: A good apology is like an antibiotic; a bad apology is like rubbing salt in the wound.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: The best way to teach somebody something is to have them think they're learning something else.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: If you took one-tenth the energy you put into complaining and applied it to solving the problem, you'd be surprised by how well things can work out.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: Luck is where preparation meets opportunity.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: Time is all you have. And you may find one day that you have less than you think.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: I am going to keep having fun every day I have left, because there is no other way of life. You just have to decide whether you're a Tigger or an Eeyore.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: Complaining does not work as a strategy. We all have finite time and energy. Any time we spend whining is unlikely to help us achieve our goals. And it won't make us happier.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: You can't get there alone. People have to help you, and I do believe in karma. I'll take an earnest person over a hip person every day, because hip is short-term. Earnest is long-term.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: The questions are always more important than the answers.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: Find the best in everybody. Just keep waiting no matter how long it takes. No one is all evil. Everybody has a good side, just keep waiting, it will come out.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: When there's an elephant in the room, introduce him.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: Showing gratitude is one of the simplest yet most powerful things humans can do for each other.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: We don't beat the Reaper by living longer, we beat the Reaper by living well and living fully.
author: Randy Pausch — The Last Lecture

text: You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backward. So you have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future.
author: Steve Jobs — Stanford Commencement 2005

text: Getting fired from Apple was the best thing that could have ever happened to me. The heaviness of being successful was replaced by the lightness of being a beginner again.
author: Steve Jobs — Stanford Commencement 2005

text: I'm convinced that the only thing that kept me going was that I loved what I did. You've got to find what you love. If you haven't found it yet, keep looking. Don't settle.
author: Steve Jobs — Stanford Commencement 2005

text: Don't be trapped by dogma — which is living with the results of other people's thinking. Don't let the noise of others' opinions drown out your own inner voice.
author: Steve Jobs — Stanford Commencement 2005

text: Do, or do not. There is no 'try.'
author: Yoda — Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

text: Fear is the path to the dark side. Fear leads to anger. Anger leads to hate. Hate leads to suffering.
author: Yoda — Star Wars: The Phantom Menace

text: Your focus determines your reality.
author: Qui-Gon Jinn — Star Wars: The Phantom Menace

text: Hope is like the sun. If you only believe in it when you can see it, you'll never make it through the night.
author: General Leia — Star Wars

text: You got a dream, you gotta protect it. When people can't do something themselves, they're gonna tell you that you can't do it. You want something, go get it. Period.
author: Chris Gardner — The Pursuit of Happyness

text: It ain't about how hard you hit. It's about how hard you can get hit and keep moving forward.
author: Rocky Balboa — Rocky Balboa

text: Every champion was once a contender who refused to give up.
author: Rocky Balboa — Rocky

text: The world ain't all sunshine and rainbows. It's a very mean and nasty place and I don't care how tough you are it will beat you to your knees and keep you there permanently if you let it.
author: Rocky Balboa — Rocky Balboa

text: Get busy living, or get busy dying.
author: Andy Dufresne — The Shawshank Redemption

text: Remember, Red, hope is a good thing, maybe the best of things, and no good thing ever dies.
author: Andy Dufresne — The Shawshank Redemption

text: My mama always said life was like a box of chocolates. You never know what you're gonna get.
author: Forrest Gump — Forrest Gump

text: It's not who I am underneath, but what I do that defines me.
author: Bruce Wayne — Batman Begins

text: I'm trying to free your mind, Neo. But I can only show you the door. You're the one that has to walk through it.
author: Morpheus — The Matrix

text: What we do in life echoes in eternity.
author: Maximus — Gladiator

text: A day may come when the courage of men fails, but it is not this day.
author: Aragorn — The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

text: You mustn't be afraid to dream a little bigger, darling.
author: Eames — Inception

text: The smallest seed of an idea can grow. It can grow to define or destroy you.
author: Cobb — Inception

text: You'll have bad times, but it'll always wake you up to the good stuff you weren't paying attention to.
author: Sean Maguire — Good Will Hunting

text: Real loss is only possible when you love something more than you love yourself.
author: Sean Maguire — Good Will Hunting

text: Carpe diem. Seize the day, boys. Make your lives extraordinary.
author: John Keating — Dead Poets Society

text: We don't read and write poetry because it's cute. We read and write poetry because we are members of the human race. And the human race is filled with passion.
author: John Keating — Dead Poets Society

text: No matter what anybody tells you, words and ideas can change the world.
author: John Keating — Dead Poets Society

text: Oh yes, the past can hurt. But the way I see it, you can either run from it or learn from it.
author: Rafiki — The Lion King

text: It's only after we've lost everything that we're free to do anything.
author: Tyler Durden — Fight Club

text: The things you own end up owning you.
author: Tyler Durden — Fight Club

text: You're not your job. You're not how much money you have in the bank. You're not the car you drive. You're not the contents of your wallet.
author: Tyler Durden — Fight Club

text: A man who doesn't spend time with his family can never be a real man.
author: Vito Corleone — The Godfather

text: Keep your friends close, but your enemies closer.
author: Michael Corleone — The Godfather Part II

text: Every man dies, not every man really lives.
author: William Wallace — Braveheart

text: They may take our lives, but they'll never take our freedom!
author: William Wallace — Braveheart

text: We used to look up at the sky and wonder at our place in the stars. Now we just look down, and worry about our place in the dirt.
author: Cooper — Interstellar

text: Love is the one thing we're capable of perceiving that transcends dimensions of time and space.
author: Dr. Brand — Interstellar

text: Mankind was born on Earth. It was never meant to die here.
author: Cooper — Interstellar

text: Whoever saves one life, saves the world entire.
author: Oskar Schindler — Schindler's List

text: Beneath this mask there is more than flesh. Beneath this mask there is an idea, and ideas are bulletproof.
author: V — V for Vendetta

text: Not everyone can become a great artist, but a great artist can come from anywhere.
author: Anton Ego — Ratatouille

text: Just keep swimming.
author: Dory — Finding Nemo

text: Our deepest fear is not that we are inadequate. Our deepest fear is that we are powerful beyond measure. It is our light, not our darkness, that most frightens us.
author: Timo Cruz — Coach Carter

text: There are no accidents.
author: Master Oogway — Kung Fu Panda

text: I know what I have to do now. I've got to keep breathing because tomorrow the sun will rise. Who knows what the tide could bring?
author: Chuck Noland — Cast Away

text: We're all pretty bizarre. Some of us are just better at hiding it, that's all.
author: Andrew Clark — The Breakfast Club

text: Perhaps it is good to have a beautiful mind, but an even greater gift is to discover a beautiful heart.
author: John Nash — A Beautiful Mind

text: There's a dream that was Rome. You could only whisper it. Anything more than a whisper and it would vanish, it was so fragile.
author: Marcus Aurelius — Gladiator

text: कर्म करो, फल की चिंता मत करो।
author: भगवद्गीता — अध्याय २.४७

text: तुम्हें कर्म करने का अधिकार है, फलों का नहीं।
author: भगवद्गीता — २.४७

text: जो हुआ, अच्छा हुआ। जो हो रहा है, अच्छा हो रहा है। जो होगा, वह भी अच्छा होगा।
author: भगवद्गीता

text: मनुष्य अपने विश्वास से निर्मित होता है। जैसा वह विश्वास करता है, वैसा वह बन जाता है।
author: भगवद्गीता — १७.३

text: आत्मा न कभी जन्म लेती है, न मरती है। यह शाश्वत, अजर और अमर है।
author: भगवद्गीता — २.२०

text: ध्यान से ज्ञान आता है, ध्यान की कमी से अज्ञान। इसलिए जानो कि तुम्हें किस मार्ग पर आगे बढ़ना है।
author: भगवद्गीता — २.६६

text: योग कर्मसु कौशलम् — कुशलता से किया गया कर्म ही योग है।
author: भगवद्गीता — २.५०

text: जो मन को वश में कर लेता है, उसके लिए मन ही परम मित्र है। जो नहीं कर पाता, उसके लिए मन ही शत्रु बन जाता है।
author: भगवद्गीता — ६.६

text: खुद को जीतना ही सबसे बड़ी जीत है।
author: भगवद्गीता

text: श्रद्धावान लभते ज्ञानम् — श्रद्धा रखने वाला व्यक्ति ज्ञान प्राप्त करता है।
author: भगवद्गीता — ४.३९

text: दुःख में सुमिरन सब करें, सुख में करे न कोय। जो सुख में सुमिरन करे, दुःख काहे को होय।
author: कबीरदास

text: बुरा जो देखन मैं चला, बुरा न मिलिया कोय। जो दिल खोजा आपना, मुझसे बुरा न कोय।।
author: कबीरदास

text: काल करे सो आज कर, आज करे सो अब। पल में प्रलय होएगी, बहुरि करेगा कब।।
author: कबीरदास

text: माटी कहे कुम्हार से, तू क्या रौंदे मोहि। एक दिन ऐसा आएगा, मैं रौंदूंगी तोहि।।
author: कबीरदास

text: पोथी पढ़ि पढ़ि जग मुआ, पंडित भया न कोय। ढाई आखर प्रेम का, पढ़े सो पंडित होय।।
author: कबीरदास

text: जाति न पूछो साधु की, पूछ लीजिए ज्ञान। मोल करो तलवार का, पड़ा रहन दो म्यान।।
author: कबीरदास

text: साँच बराबर तप नहीं, झूठ बराबर पाप। जाके हृदय साँच है, ताके हृदय आप।।
author: कबीरदास

text: तिनका कबहुँ न निंदिए, जो पाँव तले होय। कबहुँ उड़ि आँखिन पड़े, तो पीर घनेरी होय।।
author: कबीरदास

text: मन के हारे हार है, मन के जीते जीत। पारब्रह्म को पाइए, मन ही की परतीत।।
author: कबीरदास

text: धीरे धीरे रे मना, धीरे सब कुछ होय। माली सींचे सौ घड़ा, ऋतु आए फल होय।।
author: कबीरदास

text: अति का भला न बोलना, अति की भली न चूप। अति का भला न बरसना, अति की भली न धूप।।
author: कबीरदास

text: रात गँवाई सोय के, दिवस गँवाया खाय। हीरा जनम अमोल था, कौड़ी बदले जाय।।
author: कबीरदास

text: जाकी रही भावना जैसी, प्रभु मूरत देखी तिन तैसी।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: सुखी मीन जहाँ नीर अगाधा, जिमि हरि-भक्त जहाँ साधु-साधा।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: बिनु सत्संग विवेक न होई, राम कृपा बिनु सुलभ न सोई।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: सकल पदारथ हैं जग माहीं, करमहीन नर पावत नाहीं।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: नाम लेत भव सिंधु सुखाहीं, करहुँ विचार सुजन मन माहीं।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: धीरज, धर्म, मित्र अरु नारी। आपद काल परखिए चारी।।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास — रामचरितमानस

text: सांसारिक दुखों की ओषधि एकमात्र राम-नाम है।
author: गोस्वामी तुलसीदास

text: हज़ारों ख्वाहिशें ऐसी कि हर ख्वाहिश पे दम निकले, बहुत निकले मेरे अरमान, लेकिन फिर भी कम निकले।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: जब तक दिल में दम है, जाम का दौर चलता रहेगा।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: मैं उनका हूँ, वो मेरे हैं — यही अफ़साना मेरा है।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: इश्क पर ज़ोर नहीं, है यह वो आतश ग़ालिब। जो लगाए न लगे और बुझाए न बुझे।।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: दिल नादाँ तुझे हुआ क्या है? आखिर इस दर्द की दवा क्या है?
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: हुई मुद्दत कि ग़ालिब मर गया पर याद आता है। वो हर इक बात पर कहना कि यूँ होता तो क्या होता।।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: बाज़ीचा-ए-अत्फ़ाल है दुनिया मेरे आगे, होता है शब-ओ-रोज़ तमाशा मेरे आगे।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: रंज से ख़ूगर हुआ इन्सान तो मिट जाता है रंज। मुश्किलें मुझ पर पड़ीं इतनी कि आसाँ हो गयीं।।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: न था कुछ तो ख़ुदा था, कुछ न होता तो ख़ुदा होता। डुबोया मुझको होने ने, न होता मैं तो क्या होता।।
author: मिर्ज़ा ग़ालिब

text: रहिमन धागा प्रेम का, मत तोड़ो चटकाय। टूटे से फिर ना जुड़े, जुड़े गाँठ पड़ जाय।।
author: रहीमदास

text: बड़े काम ओछे करहिं, तो न बड़ाई होय। ज्यों रहीम हनुमंत को, गिरिधर कहत न कोय।।
author: रहीमदास

text: जो रहीम उत्तम प्रकृति, का करि सकत कुसंग। चंदन विष व्यापत नहीं, लपटे रहत भुजंग।।
author: रहीमदास

text: एकै साधे सब सधे, सब साधे सब जाय। रहिमन मूलहिं सींचिबो, फूलहिं फलहिं अघाय।।
author: रहीमदास

text: खीरा सिर ते काटिए, मलियत नमक लगाय। रहिमन करुए मुखन को, चहिए यही सजाय।।
author: रहीमदास

text: शिक्षा सबसे अच्छी मित्र है। एक शिक्षित व्यक्ति हर जगह सम्मान पाता है।
author: चाणक्य — अर्थशास्त्र

text: पहले पाँच वर्ष बच्चे को प्यार से, फिर दस वर्ष अनुशासन से, और सोलह वर्ष की आयु में मित्र की तरह पालें।
author: चाणक्य — नीतिशास्त्र

text: व्यक्ति अकेले पैदा होता है और अकेले मर जाता है। वह अकेले ही अपने कर्मों का अनुभव करता है — अच्छे या बुरे।
author: चाणक्य

text: जो व्यक्ति अपने लक्ष्य पर केंद्रित रहता है, उसके लिए कोई बाधा नहीं होती।
author: चाणक्य

text: कोई भी काम शुरू करने से पहले तीन सवाल खुद से पूछो — मैं यह क्यों कर रहा हूँ? इसका परिणाम क्या होगा? क्या मैं सफल होऊँगा?
author: चाणक्य

text: समय और चींटी का इंतज़ार कोई नहीं कर सकता।
author: चाणक्य

text: जब तक दुश्मन की कमजोरी न जानो, शांति बनाए रखो।
author: चाणक्य — अर्थशास्त्र

text: उठो, जागो और तब तक मत रुको जब तक लक्ष्य की प्राप्ति न हो जाए।
author: स्वामी विवेकानंद

text: शक्ति ही जीवन है, कमज़ोरी ही मृत्यु है।
author: स्वामी विवेकानंद

text: खुद पर विश्वास रखो। सारी शक्ति तुम्हारे भीतर है।
author: स्वामी विवेकानंद

text: ब्रह्मांड की सारी शक्तियाँ पहले से हमारी हैं। वो हमीं हैं जो अपनी आँखों पर हाथ रख लेते हैं और फिर रोते हैं कि कितना अँधेरा है।
author: स्वामी विवेकानंद

text: जो आग दूसरों को गर्मी देती है, उसमें जलने की ताकत भी होती है।
author: स्वामी विवेकानंद

text: जो बीत गई सो बात गई, जीवन में एक सितारा था, माना वह बेहद प्यारा था। वह डूब गया तो डूब गया।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन

text: मन का हो तो अच्छा, न हो तो और भी अच्छा।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन

text: जीवन की आपाधापी में कब वक़्त मिला, कुछ देर कहीं पर बैठ कभी यह सोच सकूँ।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन

text: लहरों से डरकर नौका पार नहीं होती, कोशिश करने वालों की कभी हार नहीं होती।
author: सोहनलाल द्विवेदी

text: जो हो चुका है उस पर आँसू बहाना छोड़ो, जो आने वाला है उसे सपनों में सँजो लो।
author: माखनलाल चतुर्वेदी

text: सर्वे भवन्तु सुखिनः। सर्वे सन्तु निरामयाः। सर्वे भद्राणि पश्यन्तु। मा कश्चित् दुःखभाग् भवेत्।।
author: बृहदारण्यक उपनिषद् — प्राचीन प्रार्थना

text: तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय। मृत्योर्मामृतं गमय।
author: बृहदारण्यक उपनिषद् — १.३.२८

text: अहं ब्रह्मास्मि — मैं ब्रह्म हूँ।
author: बृहदारण्यक उपनिषद् — १.४.१०

text: तत् त्वम् असि — वह तुम हो।
author: छान्दोग्य उपनिषद् — ६.८.७

text: वसुधैव कुटुम्बकम् — सारी पृथ्वी एक परिवार है।
author: महोपनिषद् — ६.७२

text: यत्र नार्यस्तु पूज्यन्ते, रमन्ते तत्र देवताः। — जहाँ नारी की पूजा होती है, वहाँ देवता निवास करते हैं।
author: मनुस्मृति — ३.५६

text: विद्या ददाति विनयम् — ज्ञान विनम्रता देता है।
author: हितोपदेश

text: उद्यमेन हि सिध्यन्ति कार्याणि न मनोरथैः। न हि सुप्तस्य सिंहस्य प्रविशन्ति मुखे मृगाः।।
author: हितोपदेश — सुप्त सिंह को शिकार नहीं मिलता

text:
वृक्ष हों भले खड़े,
हों घने हों बड़े,
एक पत्र छांह भी,
मांग मत, मांग मत, मांग मत,
अग्निपथ अग्निपथ अग्निपथ।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन — अग्निपथ (प्रथम पद)

text:
तू न थकेगा कभी,
तू न रुकेगा कभी,
तू न मुड़ेगा कभी,
कर शपथ, कर शपथ, कर शपथ,
अग्निपथ अग्निपथ अग्निपथ।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन — अग्निपथ (द्वितीय पद)

text:
यह महान दृश्य है,
चल रहा मनुष्य है,
अश्रु श्वेत रक्त से,
लथपथ लथपथ लथपथ,
अग्निपथ अग्निपथ अग्निपथ।
author: हरिवंशराय बच्चन — अग्निपथ (तृतीय पद)

text: I don't want to conquer anything. I just think that the guy with the most freedom in this whole ocean... is the Pirate King!
author: Monkey D. Luffy (One Piece)

text: If you don't risk your life, you can't create a future. Right?
author: Monkey D. Luffy (One Piece)

text: Our motto on our hard days of training: 'To be stronger than yesterday.' If I have to I'll be stronger than a half day ago, even a minute ago.
author: Rock Lee (Naruto)

text: A genius, huh? What does that mean? 'Genius'? So I was not born with a whole lot of natural talent, not gifted like Neji... but I work hard and I never give up! That is my gift; that is my ninja way!
author: Rock Lee (Naruto)

text: You're right, all efforts are pointless... If you don't believe in yourself.
author: Might Guy (Naruto)

text: You have the gift of perseverance, and that's what makes you a genius too!
author: Might Guy (Naruto)

text: Don't let anything stir you up the path you have drawn for yourself! Forge ahead in the end! Stick to it Lee! Make me proud! Be everything you can be!
author: Might Guy (Naruto)

text: If you believe in your dreams, I will prove to you, that you can achieve your dreams just by working hard.
author: Rock Lee (Naruto)

text: What are you doing? Why do you run Ichigo? You still have not called me. Face forward, Ichigo. You should be able to hear it now. That which blocks your ears is worthless fear. The enemy is one, you are one. What is there to fear? Cast off your fear. Look forward! Go forward! Never stand still. Retreat and you will age. Hesitate and you will die. Shout... my name is... ZANGETSU!
author: Zangetsu (Bleach)

text: A lesson without pain is meaningless. For you cannot gain anything without sacrificing something else in return, but once you have overcome it and made it your own, you will gain an irreplaceable fullmetal heart.
author: Edward Elric (Fullmetal Alchemist)

text: It's a cruel and random world, but the chaos is all so beautiful.
author: Roy Mustang (Fullmetal Alchemist)

text: Even when our eyes are closed, there's a whole world that exists outside ourselves and our dreams.
author: Alphonse Elric (Fullmetal Alchemist)

text: Stand up and walk. Keep moving forward. You've got two good legs. So get up and use them. You're strong enough to make your own path.
author: Edward Elric (Fullmetal Alchemist)

text: If you can't find a door, make your own.
author: Izumi Curtis (Fullmetal Alchemist)

text: Someone who can't sacrifice anything, can't change anything. To defeat a monster, you must be willing to throw aside your humanity and all that makes you human.
author: Armin Arlert (Attack on Titan)

text: The difference between your decision and ours is experience. But you don't have to rely on that. Choose. Believe in yourself, or believe in the Survey Corps and me. I don't know the answer. I never have. Whether you trust in your own strength or trust in the choices made by reliable comrades. No one knows what the outcome will be.
author: Levi Ackerman (Attack on Titan)

text: This world is cruel. And yet, so beautiful.
author: Mikasa Ackerman (Attack on Titan)

text: When I say it doesn't hurt me, that means I can bear it.
author: Killua Zoldyck (Hunter x Hunter)

text: You believe I cannot pray with a single arm? A prayer comes from the heart. If the heart achieves the correct form, it becomes emotion, and if the body achieves the correct form, it becomes gratitude.
author: Isaac Netero (Hunter x Hunter)

text: It seems you have something you want to protect. When that is threatened, your true strength will show itself.
author: Meruem (Hunter x Hunter)

text: Whether you win or lose, you can always come out ahead by surpassing yourself.
author: All Might (My Hero Academia)

text: A hero is someone who never gives up, who keeps on fighting till the end!
author: All Might (My Hero Academia)

text: I'll do it. I'll become a hero!
author: Shoto Todoroki (My Hero Academia)

text: Meddling when you don't need to is the essence of being a hero.
author: Tenya Iida (My Hero Academia)

text: Keep your heart burning, and do your best. Push yourself to the limit!
author: Tanjiro Kamado (Demon Slayer)

text: Those who regret their own actions, I will not trample on their souls. Because demons were once human, too. Just like me, they were human too.
author: Giyu Tomioka (Demon Slayer)

text: Growing old and dying is what gives meaning and beauty to the fleeting span of a human life. It's precisely because we age and die that our lives have value and nobility.
author: Kyojuro Rengoku (Demon Slayer)

text: Set your heart ablaze!
author: Kyojuro Rengoku (Demon Slayer)

text: No matter how many people you may lose, you have no choice but to go on living. No matter how devastating the blows may be.
author: Tanjiro Kamado (Demon Slayer)

text: Push through the pain, giving up hurts more.
author: Vegeta (Dragon Ball Z)

text: It is not a sin to fight for the right cause. There are those who words alone will not reach.
author: Master Roshi (Dragon Ball Z)

text: There are many types of monsters in this world. Monsters who will not show themselves and who cause trouble. Monsters who abduct children. Monsters who devour dreams. Monsters who suck blood. And monsters who always tell lies.
author: L Lawliet (Death Note)

text: I'll take a potato chip and eat it!
author: Light Yagami (Death Note)

text: If you use your head, you won't get fat even if you eat sweets.
author: L Lawliet (Death Note)

text: It's not the world that's messed up; it's those of us in it. Yes, some ghouls walk a path that leaves sorrow in their wake, but just like humans, we can choose a different path altogether.
author: Ken Kaneki (Tokyo Ghoul)

text: Sometimes good people make bad choices. It doesn't mean they are bad people. It means they're human.
author: Touka Kirishima (Tokyo Ghoul)

text: If you were to write a story with me in the lead role, it would certainly be a tragedy.
author: Ken Kaneki (Tokyo Ghoul)

text: False tears bring pain to those around you. A false smile brings pain to oneself.
author: Lelouch Lamperouge (Code Geass)

text: You can't change the world without getting your hands dirty.
author: C.C. (Code Geass)

text: Whatever happens, happens.
author: Spike Spiegel (Cowboy Bebop)

text: The past is the past and the future is the future. A man is a man and a woman is a woman. The present is the present. I am who I am and you are who you are. That's all there is to it. Does it really matter? Or do we just think it does?
author: Spike Spiegel (Cowboy Bebop)

text: Everything has a beginning and an end. Life is just a cycle of starts and stops. There are ends we don't desire, but they're inevitable, we have to face them. It's what being human is all about.
author: Jet Black (Cowboy Bebop)

text: I'm just a wanderer. I go where the wind takes me.
author: Jubei Kibagami (Ninja Scroll)

text: Even a stone Buddha has feelings.
author: Dakuan (Ninja Scroll)

text: I am a tool of the Koga ninja clan. My life has no other purpose.
author: Kagero (Ninja Scroll)

text: I've seen enough killing. I just want to be left alone.
author: Jubei Kibagami (Ninja Scroll)

text: Even if my body is poisoned, my heart remains pure.
author: Kagero (Ninja Scroll)

text: In this world, there are things that cannot be explained by reason alone.
author: Dakuan (Ninja Scroll)

text: Immortality is not a gift, but a curse that binds you to this world.
author: Gemma Himuro (Ninja Scroll)

text: I'd rather trust and regret than doubt and regret.
author: Kirito (Sword Art Online)

text: Real pain is the pain of not knowing what to do. Real pain is the pain of not knowing what's right.
author: Asuna (Sword Art Online)

text: People's feelings are memories that transcend time.
author: Rintaro Okabe (Steins;Gate)

text: Time is passing so quickly. Right now, I feel like complaining to Einstein. Whether time is slow or fast depends on perception. Relativity theory is so romantic. And so sad.
author: Kurisu Makise (Steins;Gate)

text: The opposite of love isn't hate, it's apathy.
author: Kyoko Hoin (Kodomo no Jikan)

text: I'm just a guy who's a hero for fun.
author: Saitama (One Punch Man)

text: If everyone is not special, maybe you can be what you want to be.
author: Mob (Mob Psycho 100)

text: Being the best decoy ever is as cool as being the ace.
author: Shoyo Hinata (Haikyu!!)

text: Whether a fish lives in a clear stream or a water ditch, so long as it continues swimming forward, it will grow up beautifully.
author: Koro-Sensei (Assassination Classroom)

text: We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Strength does not come from physical capacity. It comes from an indomitable will.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: The future depends on what you do today.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Life is like riding a bicycle. To keep your balance, you must keep moving.
author: Albert Einstein

text: Imagination is more important than knowledge. For knowledge is limited, whereas imagination embraces the entire world.
author: Albert Einstein

text: You may not control all the events that happen to you, but you can decide not to be reduced by them.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The only thing worse than being blind is having sight but no vision.
author: Helen Keller

text: Alone we can do so little; together we can do so much.
author: Helen Keller

text: Tell me and I forget. Teach me and I remember. Involve me and I learn.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: Whatever you are, be a good one.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: There is the heat of Love, the pulsing rush of Longing, the lover's whisper, irresistible—magic to make the sanest man go mad.
author: Homer (The Iliad)

text: Of all creatures that breathe and move upon the earth, nothing is bred that is weaker than man.
author: Homer (The Odyssey)

text: There is nothing more admirable than when two people who see eye to eye keep house as man and wife, confounding their enemies and delighting their friends.
author: Homer (The Odyssey)

text: All men make mistakes, but a good man yields when he knows his course is wrong, and repairs the evil. The only crime is pride.
author: Sophocles (Antigone)

text: How dreadful knowledge of the truth can be when there's no help in truth!
author: Sophocles (Oedipus Rex)

text: He who learns must suffer. And even in our sleep pain that cannot forget falls drop by drop upon the heart, and in our own despair, against our will, comes wisdom to us by the awful grace of God.
author: Aeschylus (Agamemnon)

text: There is no worse enemy than a former friend.
author: Euripides (Medea)

text: The price good men pay for indifference to public affairs is to be ruled by evil men.
author: Plato (The Republic)

text: No man ever steps in the same river twice, for it's not the same river and he's not the same man.
author: Heraclitus

text: Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not; remember that what you now have was once among the things you only hoped for.
author: Epicurus

text: The contact between the senses and the sense objects gives rise to fleeting perceptions of happiness and distress. These are non-permanent, and come and go like the winter and summer seasons. O descendent of Bharat, one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.
author: Bhagavad Gita (Chapter 2, Verse 14)

text: Wherever there is Krishna, the master of all mystics, and wherever there is Arjuna, the supreme archer, there will also certainly be opulence, victory, extraordinary power, and morality.
author: Bhagavad Gita (Chapter 18, Verse 78)

text: Arise! Awake! Approach the great and learn. Like the sharp edge of a razor is that path, so the wise say—hard to tread and difficult to cross.
author: Katha Upanishad

text: Tat Tvam Asi (Thou art That)—You are the ultimate reality.
author: Chandogya Upanishad

text: Let yourself be silently drawn by the strange pull of what you really love. It will not lead you astray.
author: Rumi

text: Raise your words, not voice. It is rain that grows flowers, not thunder.
author: Rumi

text: You can't cross the sea merely by standing and staring at the water.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: Where the mind is without fear and the head is held high; Where knowledge is free.
author: Rabindranath Tagore (Gitanjali)

text: Let your life lightly dance on the edges of Time like dew on the tip of a leaf.
author: Rabindranath Tagore

text: Two roads diverged in a wood, and I—I took the one less traveled by, and that has made all the difference.
author: Robert Frost (The Road Not Taken)

text: In three words I can sum up everything I've learned about life: it goes on.
author: Robert Frost

text: You may trod me in the very dirt, but still, like dust, I'll rise.
author: Maya Angelou (Still I Rise)

text: Does my sassiness upset you? Why are you beset with gloom? 'Cause I walk like I've got oil wells pumping in my living room.
author: Maya Angelou (Still I Rise)

text: I am the master of my fate, I am the captain of my soul.
author: William Ernest Henley (Invictus)

text: Out of the night that covers me, black as the pit from pole to pole, I thank whatever gods may be for my unconquerable soul.
author: William Ernest Henley (Invictus)

text: Do not go gentle into that good night. Rage, rage against the dying of the light.
author: Dylan Thomas

text: The world is too much with us; late and soon, getting and spending, we lay waste our powers.
author: William Wordsworth

text: For oft, when on my couch I lie in vacant or in pensive mood, they flash upon that inward eye which is the bliss of solitude.
author: William Wordsworth (I Wandered Lonely as a Cloud)

text: Hope is the thing with feathers that perches in the soul—and sings the tunes without the words—and never stops at all.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Forever is composed of nows.
author: Emily Dickinson

text: Do I dare disturb the universe?
author: T.S. Eliot (The Love Song of J. Alfred Prufrock)

text: Only those who will risk going too far can possibly find out how far one can go.
author: T.S. Eliot

text: Hold fast to dreams, for if dreams die, life is a broken-winged bird that cannot fly.
author: Langston Hughes (Dreams)

text: Life for me ain't been no crystal stair.
author: Langston Hughes (Mother to Son)

text: Do I contradict myself? Very well then I contradict myself, (I am large, I contain multitudes.)
author: Walt Whitman (Song of Myself)

text: That you are here—that life exists and identity, that the powerful play goes on, and you may contribute a verse.
author: Walt Whitman (O Me! O Life!)

text: If you can meet with Triumph and Disaster and treat those two impostors just the same... yours is the Earth and everything that's in it.
author: Rudyard Kipling (If)

text: To strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield.
author: Alfred, Lord Tennyson (Ulysses)

text: 'Tis better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all.
author: Alfred, Lord Tennyson

text: All the world's a stage, and all the men and women merely players.
author: William Shakespeare (As You Like It)

text: We are such stuff as dreams are made on, and our little life is rounded with a sleep.
author: William Shakespeare (The Tempest)

text: Above all, don't lie to yourself. The man who lies to himself and listens to his own lie comes to a point that he cannot distinguish the truth within him, or around him, and so loses all respect for himself and for others.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky (The Brothers Karamazov)

text: Pain and suffering are always inevitable for a large intelligence and a deep heart.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky (Crime and Punishment)

text: The mystery of human existence lies not in just staying alive, but in finding something to live for.
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky

text: All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.
author: Leo Tolstoy (Anna Karenina)

text: We can know only that we know nothing. And that is the highest degree of human wisdom.
author: Leo Tolstoy (War and Peace)

text: Everyone thinks of changing the world, but no one thinks of changing himself.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: Even the darkest night will end and the sun will rise.
author: Victor Hugo (Les Misérables)

text: To love another person is to see the face of God.
author: Victor Hugo (Les Misérables)

text: Music expresses that which cannot be said and on which it is impossible to be silent.
author: Victor Hugo

text: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times.
author: Charles Dickens (A Tale of Two Cities)

text: Take nothing on its looks; take everything on evidence. There's no better rule.
author: Charles Dickens (Great Expectations)

text: It is not down on any map; true places never are.
author: Herman Melville (Moby-Dick)

text: I try all things, I achieve what I can.
author: Herman Melville (Moby-Dick)

text: A book must be the axe for the frozen sea within us.
author: Franz Kafka

text: Paths are made by walking.
author: Franz Kafka

text: The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart. One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
author: Albert Camus (The Myth of Sisyphus)

text: I may not have been sure about what really did interest me, but I was absolutely sure about what didn't.
author: Albert Camus (The Stranger)

text: Twenty years from now you will be more disappointed by the things that you didn't do than by the ones you did do.
author: Mark Twain

text: You are never too old to set another goal or to dream a new dream.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Courage is not simply one of the virtues, but the form of every virtue at the testing point.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: If you want to keep a secret, you must also hide it from yourself.
author: George Orwell (1984)

text: All animals are equal, but some animals are more equal than others.
author: George Orwell (Animal Farm)

text: There is a stubbornness about me that never can bear to be frightened at the will of others. My courage always rises at every attempt to intimidate me.
author: Jane Austen (Pride and Prejudice)

text: We are all fools in love.
author: Jane Austen (Persuasion)

text: For what it's worth: it's never too late or, in my case, too early to be whoever you want to be.
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald

text: But man is not made for defeat. A man can be destroyed but not defeated.
author: Ernest Hemingway (The Old Man and the Sea)

text: The world breaks everyone, and afterward, some are strong at the broken places.
author: Ernest Hemingway

text: A man of genius makes no mistakes. His errors are volitional and are the portals of discovery.
author: James Joyce (Ulysses)

text: You cannot find peace by avoiding life.
author: Virginia Woolf

text: She had the perpetual sense, as she watched the taxi cabs, of being out, out, far out to sea and alone; she always had the feeling that it was very, very dangerous to live even one day.
author: Virginia Woolf (Mrs. Dalloway)

text: The brick walls are there for a reason. The brick walls are not there to keep us out. The brick walls are there to give us a chance to show how badly we want something. Because the brick walls are there to stop the people who don't want it badly enough. They're there to stop the other people.
author: Randy Pausch

text: We cannot change the cards we are dealt, just how we play the hand.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Experience is what you get when you didn't get what you wanted. And experience is often the most valuable thing you have to offer.
author: Randy Pausch

text: It's not about how to achieve your dreams, it's about how to lead your life. If you lead your life the right way, the karma will take care of itself, the dreams will come to you.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Never lose the childlike wonder. Show gratitude. Don't complain; just work harder. Never give up.
author: Randy Pausch

text: When you're screwing up and nobody says anything to you anymore, that means they've given up on you.
author: Randy Pausch

text: The key question to keep asking is, Are you spending your time on the right things? Because time is all you have.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Proper apologies have three parts: 1) What I did was wrong. 2) I'm sorry that I hurt you. 3) How do I make it better? It's the third part that people tend to forget.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Be good at something; it makes you valuable. Have something to bring to the table, because that will make you more welcome.
author: Randy Pausch

text: If you wait long enough, people will surprise and impress you.
author: Randy Pausch

text: No job is beneath you. You ought to be thrilled you got a job in the mailroom. And when you get there, here's what you do: Be really great at sorting mail.
author: Randy Pausch

text: I've always believed that if you took one-tenth the energy you put into complaining and applied it to solving the problem, you'd be surprised by how well things can work out.
author: Randy Pausch

text: When you see yourself doing something badly and nobody's bothering to tell you anymore, that's a bad place to be. You may not want to hear it, but your critics are often the ones telling you they still love you and care about you, and want to make you better.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Another way to be prepared is to think negatively. Yes, I'm a great optimist. But, when trying to make a decision, I often think of the worst-case scenario. I call it 'the eaten by wolves factor.' If I do something, what's the most terrible thing that could happen? Would I be eaten by wolves? One thing that makes it possible to be an optimist is if you have a contingency plan for when all hell breaks out.
author: Randy Pausch

text: You can't get there alone. People have to help you, and I do believe in karma. I believe in paybacks. You get people to help you by telling the truth. Being earnest. I'll take an earnest person over a hip person every day, because hip is short-term. Earnest is long-term.
author: Randy Pausch

text: Whether you think you can or can't, you're right.
author: Randy Pausch

text: I don't believe in the no-win scenario.
author: Randy Pausch

text: In a dark place we find ourselves, and a little more knowledge lights our way.
author: Yoda (Star Wars)

text: Your eyes can deceive you. Don't trust them.
author: Obi-Wan Kenobi (Star Wars)

text: Never give up on your dream, fight your hardest for it. Don't ever let somebody tell you you can't do something, not even me. Alright? You dream, you gotta protect it. People can't do something themselves, they wanna tell you you can't do it. If you want something, go get it. Period.
author: Chris Gardner (The Pursuit of Happyness)

text: Stupid is as stupid does.
author: Forrest Gump

text: You have to do the best with what God gave you.
author: Mrs. Gump (Forrest Gump)

text: Why do we fall? So we can learn to pick ourselves up.
author: Alfred Pennyworth (The Dark Knight)

text: The night is darkest just before the dawn. And I promise you, the dawn is coming.
author: Harvey Dent (The Dark Knight)

text: What is real? How do you define real? If you're talking about what you can feel, what you can smell, what you can taste and see, then real is simply electrical signals interpreted by your brain.
author: Morpheus (The Matrix)

text: Know thyself.
author: The Oracle (The Matrix)

text: There's some good in this world, Mr. Frodo, and it's worth fighting for.
author: Samwise Gamgee (The Lord of the Rings)

text: You're waiting for a train. A train that will take you far away. You know where you hope this train will take you, but you can't be sure.
author: Cobb (Inception)

text: Remember who you are. You are my son, and the one true king.
author: Mufasa (The Lion King)

text: You gotta put your behind in your past.
author: Timon (The Lion King)

text: I'm gonna make him an offer he can't refuse.
author: Don Vito Corleone (The Godfather)

text: You don't get to 500 million friends without making a few enemies.
author: Mark Zuckerberg (The Social Network)

text: Wax on, wax off.
author: Mr. Miyagi (The Karate Kid)

text: No such thing as bad student, only bad teacher.
author: Mr. Miyagi (The Karate Kid)

text: After a while, you learn to ignore the names people call you and just trust who you are.
author: Shrek

text: Anyone can cook, but only the fearless can be great.
author: Gusteau (Ratatouille)

text: Yesterday is history, tomorrow is a mystery, but today is a gift. That is why it is called the present.
author: Master Oogway (Kung Fu Panda)

text: I guess it's hard for people who are so used to things the way they are—even if they're bad—to change. 'Cause they kind of give up. And when they do, everybody kind of loses.
author: Trevor McKinney (Pay It Forward)

text: The horror, the horror.
author: Colonel Kurtz (Apocalypse Now)

text: Rosebud.
author: Charles Foster Kane (Citizen Kane)

text: Here's looking at you, kid.
author: Rick Blaine (Casablanca)

text: We'll always have Paris.
author: Rick Blaine (Casablanca)

text: Say hello to my little friend!
author: Tony Montana (Scarface)

text: The world is yours.
author: Tony Montana (Scarface)

text: I'll be back.
author: The Terminator

text: Houston, we have a problem.
author: Jim Lovell (Apollo 13)

text: Show me the money!
author: Jerry Maguire

text: You had me at hello.
author: Dorothy Boyd (Jerry Maguire)

text: You can't handle the truth!
author: Colonel Jessup (A Few Good Men)

text: You're gonna need a bigger boat.
author: Chief Brody (Jaws)

text: There's no place like home.
author: Dorothy (The Wizard of Oz)

text: After all, tomorrow is another day!
author: Scarlett O'Hara (Gone with the Wind)

text: If you build it, he will come.
author: Voice (Field of Dreams)

text: What we've got here is failure to communicate.
author: Captain (Cool Hand Luke)

text: Greed, for lack of a better word, is good.
author: Gordon Gekko (Wall Street)

text: You talkin' to me?
author: Travis Bickle (Taxi Driver)

text: I dropped out and trusted that it would all work out OK. It was pretty scary at the time, but looking back it was one of the best decisions I ever made.
author: Steve Jobs

text: You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backward. So you have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future. You have to trust in something—your gut, destiny, life, karma, whatever. This approach has never let me down, and it has made all the difference in my life.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Getting fired from Apple was the best thing that could have ever happened to me. The heaviness of being successful was replaced by the lightness of being a beginner again, less sure about everything. It freed me to enter one of the most creative periods of my life.
author: Steve Jobs

text: I'm convinced that the only thing that kept me going was that I loved what I did. You've got to find what you love. And that is as true for your work as it is for your lovers. Your work is going to fill a large part of your life, and the only way to be truly satisfied is to do what you believe is great work. And the only way to do great work is to love what you do. If you haven't found it yet, keep looking. Don't settle.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life. Don't be trapped by dogma—which is living with the results of other people's thinking. Don't let the noise of others' opinions drown out your own inner voice. And most important, have the courage to follow your heart and intuition. They somehow already know what you truly want to become. Everything else is secondary.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Stay Hungry. Stay Foolish.
author: The Whole Earth Catalog
